title: Schedule TEMPEST Re-certification 90 Days Early
body: Our TEMPEST recertification took 3 weeks longer than expected due to contractor availability. Lesson: schedule 90 days before expiration, not 30. The inspector's earliest available slot was 6 weeks out.
adminComment: Excellent point — recommend all sites plan 90-day lead time for TEMPEST work.

title: Pre-Inspection Walk-Through Two Weeks Prior
body: We do a complete walk-through of the SCIF using the full DCSA checklist two weeks before the annual inspection. This catches stale visitor logs, expired badges, and any physical security drift before they become formal findings. Our open findings dropped from 6 to 2 using this approach.
adminComment: Rolling this out as an enterprise standard for Q3.

title: Server Room B Shielding Gap — Emergency Procurement
body: TEMPEST scan identified an RF shielding gap in Server Room B at the cable penetration point. Root cause: contractor used non-EMC cable gland during a network upgrade last year. Corrective action: emergency procurement of EMC cable glands and professional re-seal. Contractor engaged — expected completion within 30 days.
adminComment: Tracked at corporate level. Procurement approved. Follow up in 30 days.

title: Cable Separation Documentation Must Be Re-verified After Any Wiring Work
body: A prior ICD 705 finding on cable separation was caused by an IT contractor re-routing cables without notifying the FSO. After that incident, we now require FSO sign-off on any in-SCIF wiring work, no matter how minor. If a contractor touches cables, the FSO checks separation compliance before they leave. This prevented another finding in this cycle.
adminComment: Policy change approved. Adding FSO sign-off requirement to all site SOPs.

title: Quarterly 30-Minute Mini Self-Inspections
body: We conduct quarterly 30-minute mini walk-throughs using a simplified checklist before the annual inspection. This catches drift early — things like visitor logs filling up, badge readers acting inconsistently, or personnel forgetting clean-desk procedures. We have had zero findings for 2 years running using this approach.
adminComment: Highly recommended to all sites. Adding to enterprise FSO guide.

title: No corrective actions — clean inspection
body: This inspection produced zero findings. No corrective actions required. All prior corrective actions from the previous cycle have been fully resolved and verified.
adminComment: Outstanding result. Huntsville is the enterprise benchmark.

title: Paper Visitor Logs Do Not Meet DAAPM Requirements
body: Our paper visitor log system failed to capture required data fields consistently — entries were missing authorized escort information and classification levels of areas visited. DCSA flagged this as a DAAPM non-compliance. The lesson: paper logs require constant monitoring and personnel discipline. Electronic visitor management (eLog) eliminates human error entirely and auto-generates compliant reports.
adminComment: This is the most common finding across the enterprise. eLog deployment prioritized.

title: Designate a Compliance Monitor for Each Admin System
body: Until our eLog system is deployed, we assigned a dedicated compliance monitor for visitor logs — one person responsible for reviewing entries at end of each day and correcting gaps before they accumulate. This reduced our finding risk significantly in the interim period.
adminComment: Good interim mitigation. Recommend all sites use this bridge approach.

title: eLog Electronic Visitor Management Deployment
body: DCSA finding: paper visitor logs were not compliant with DAAPM visitor tracking requirements. Corrective action: procure and deploy eLog electronic visitor management system. Procurement approved by corporate — system arriving Q3 2026. In the interim, dedicated compliance monitor assigned to daily log review.
adminComment: Procurement tracked at corporate. Target Q3 deployment for all due-soon sites.

title: TEMPEST EMI from Fluorescent Lighting
body: Older fluorescent overhead lighting in the main vault produced EMI that impacted TEMPEST scoring. We did not anticipate this during the original site build-out. Replacement with LED fixtures resolved the issue — but the procurement and installation took 4 months. Plan lighting upgrades as part of any SCIF renovation cycle.
adminComment: Flagged to facilities team for consideration in all future site renovations.

title: TEMPEST Baseline Scan After Any Facilities Work
body: After the fluorescent lighting issue we now run a quick TEMPEST baseline scan any time facilities work is done inside or adjacent to the SCIF — even minor work like painting or HVAC. This catches EMI issues before they become findings. Took about 2 hours per scan and has caught 2 issues early.
adminComment: Added to Niceville SOP. Recommend all sites adopt.

title: LED Lighting Replacement — TEMPEST EMI Remediation
body: Corrective action to resolve TEMPEST EMI finding from fluorescent lighting: full LED replacement of main vault overhead lighting. Contractor engaged — procurement completed, installation scheduled. 4-month total timeline from finding to resolution. Lesson: include LED-only specification in all future SCIF lighting requirements.

title: Dedicated Security Documentation Binder (Physical + Digital)
body: We maintain a current physical binder and a network drive folder with identical contents: all accreditation docs, NISPOM compliance evidence, training records, visitor logs (scanned), and inspection reports. During DCSA inspection the inspector was impressed — he said it saved at least 2 hours. Everything was immediately accessible without hunting through email or filing cabinets.
adminComment: Making this the enterprise standard. Sending template binder structure to all FSOs.

title: Initial Self-Inspection Documentation Backlog
body: As a new site (est. 2023), we fell behind on getting our initial self-inspection documentation complete. Contributing factors: FSO was part-time initially, no template provided, and we underestimated the documentation burden. Corrective action: corporate security team providing direct assistance, using Las Vegas binder template as starting point.
adminComment: Admin team has been assigned to directly assist Dayton through first self-inspection cycle.
